coreCompetencies: Basic understanding of what a data platform provides to data teams
Elementary knowledge of the infrastructure behind data storage and compute
Ability to provision a platform resource under guidance
Basic understanding of self-service tooling for data users
Ability to follow runbooks for platform operations
Elementary understanding of access control for data resources
Documentation of platform components and how teams use them
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with cloud storage and compute services
Basic familiarity with infrastructure-as-code concepts
Elementary understanding of how data teams consume the platform
indicators: Requires close supervision on platform tasks
Executes documented provisioning procedures
Learning how the data platform is built and operated

coreCompetencies: Ability to provision platform resources through infrastructure-as-code
Implementation of self-service templates for common data needs
Practical understanding of access control and tenancy on the platform
Ability to operate and monitor platform components
Implementation of onboarding for a data team onto the platform
Ability to expose a platform capability through internal tooling
Support of data teams consuming compute and storage
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with container orchestration for platform services
Understanding of cost visibility for platform resources
Basic understanding of platform reliability and alerting
indicators: Provisions and operates platform resources with supervision
Maintains self-service templates data teams rely on
Resolves routine platform requests

coreCompetencies: Design of self-service capabilities that data teams provision themselves
Implementation of multi-tenant isolation and access controls
Building of internal tooling and APIs over platform primitives
Definition of golden paths for common data workloads
Implementation of cost visibility and quota management per tenant
Ability to instrument the platform for reliability and usage metrics
Support of platform users through documentation and interfaces
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with policy-as-code for platform guardrails
Understanding of platform observability and service levels
Understanding of how data engineers build on the platform
indicators: Designs self-service platform capabilities
Builds internal tooling data teams adopt
Improves platform reliability and cost visibility

coreCompetencies: Architecture of a self-service data platform as an internal product
Design of multi-tenant isolation, quotas and fair resource sharing
Building of internal APIs and tooling that abstract infrastructure
Definition of golden paths that make the safe path the easy path
Implementation of platform provisioning and lifecycle automation
Design of cost allocation and chargeback across tenants
Definition of platform reliability targets and service levels
complementaryCompetencies: Understanding of the data engineering workflows the platform serves
Familiarity with governance and access policy enforcement on the platform
Understanding of capacity planning for shared infrastructure
indicators: Owns the architecture of a self-service data platform
Sets platform standards and golden paths teams follow
Leads work on multi-tenancy and cost management

coreCompetencies: Ownership of the data platform as a product across the organization
Design of self-service provisioning that scales to many data teams
Definition of platform APIs and tooling as stable internal contracts
Establishment of multi-tenancy, isolation and fair-use guarantees
Design of cost management and FinOps practices for the platform
Definition of platform reliability, service levels and on-call practices
Prioritization of platform work against the needs of data teams
complementaryCompetencies: Understanding of the boundary between platform and data engineering work
Familiarity with governance and compliance the platform enforces
Understanding of platform observability and capacity planning
indicators: Designs the data platform as a product autonomously
Runs the platform with clear service levels and cost control
Defines the multi-year platform roadmap

coreCompetencies: Definition of the data platform strategy across the organization
Architecture of self-service infrastructure for many autonomous teams
Establishment of golden paths and paved roads as the default
Definition of the platform's internal API and tooling standards
Design of cost allocation, quotas and FinOps at platform scale
Definition of reliability and service-level standards for the platform
Treatment of the platform as a product with users, roadmap and support
complementaryCompetencies: Understanding of the trade-off between flexibility and standardization
Familiarity with the platform needs of new data domains
Understanding of governance the platform must enforce for compliance
indicators: Shapes the data platform strategy across the organization
Leads adoption of paved roads by data teams
Defines the long-term platform direction

coreCompetencies: Leadership of the data platform function across the organization
Definition of platform standards and their governance
Establishment of the platform as an internal product with clear ownership
Strategy for platform reliability and self-service at organizational scale
Definition of cost transparency and efficiency across tenants
Alignment of the platform with the needs of data engineering and analytics teams
Definition of platform practices that scale with the organization
complementaryCompetencies: Understanding of platform investment trade-offs across the organization
Contribution to internal platform reference architectures
Understanding of emerging data platform practices
indicators: Sets the data platform strategy for the organization
Recognized internally as the authority on the data platform
Advises leadership on platform investment decisions

coreCompetencies: Definition of a multi-year data platform strategy
Positioning of the platform as leverage for data team productivity
Establishment of the platform organization and its operating model
Definition of platform governance across business units
Justification of platform investment through efficiency and reliability gains
Guidance of build-versus-buy decisions for platform capabilities
Leadership of innovation in self-service data infrastructure
complementaryCompetencies: Definition of platform patterns reused across the company
Understanding of the data infrastructure tooling landscape
Understanding of platform benchmarking against the industry
indicators: Owns the multi-year data platform strategy
Represents the company on data platforms in external forums
Influences platform practice beyond the organization

coreCompetencies: Executive ownership of the data platform as a business capability
Definition of the enterprise data platform vision and operating model
Accountability for platform reliability, cost efficiency and adoption
Leadership of data platform organizations across products and regions
Management of the platform budget and its return
Positioning of self-service infrastructure as an engineering advantage
Definition of the standards that keep the platform reliable at scale
complementaryCompetencies: Influence on data platform practice across the wider industry
Understanding of the platform's role in data team productivity
Understanding of long-term investment in data infrastructure
indicators: Defines and executes the enterprise data platform vision
